Package model

Class Statistics

java.lang.Object
model.Statistics
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Statistics extends Object implements Serializable
This class represents the statistics of a player in a game. It keeps track of various metrics such as games played, won, lost, guess distribution, win streaks, and calculates the win rate and average guesses.
See Also:

      public Statistics()

  • Method Details

    • updatePostGameStats

      public void updatePostGameStats(boolean isWin, int guesses)
      Updates post-game statistics based on the outcome and number of guesses.
      Parameters:
      isWin - Indicates if the game was won.
      guesses - The number of guesses taken in the game.

    • computeAverageGuesses

      public void computeAverageGuesses()
      Computes the average number of guesses per game.

      Calculates and returns the win rate.
      Returns:
      The win rate as a double.

      Gets the total number of games played.

      public int getTotalGuesses()
      Gets the total number of guesses across all games.
      Returns:
      The total number of guesses.

    • getAverageGuesses

      public int getAverageGuesses()
      Gets the average number of guesses per game.
      Returns:
      The average number of guesses.

      Gets the longest win streak.

      public int getCurrentWinStreak()
      Gets the current win streak.
      Returns:
      The current win streak.

    • getGamesLost

      public int getGamesLost()
      Gets the number of games lost.
      Returns:
      The total number of games lost.

    • getBestGame

      public int getBestGame()
      Gets the best game (fewest guesses) score.
      Returns:
      The fewest guesses taken in a winning game.

    • getWorstGame

      public int getWorstGame()
      Gets the worst game (most guesses) score.
      Returns:
      The most guesses taken in a losing game.

    • getGuessDistribution

      public Map<Integer,Integer> getGuessDistribution()
      Gets the distribution of guesses across games.
      Returns:
      A map representing the number of times each guess count has occurred.

    • getWinAtGuess

      public int[] getWinAtGuess()
      Gets the distribution of guesses across games.
      Returns:
      An array representing the number of times each guess count has occurred.
